Patch Set 3: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(1 inline comment)

Patch Set 3: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(10 inline comments)

Looks pretty good, but some minor improvements could be made.  I'm looking forward to this functionality landing.
I think it will also be quite useful for users if we can wire it to the "lfs_migrate" script (with proper group locking of the files) so it is possible to migrate files while in use.

Patch Set 3: (1 inline comment)

The MDS_SWAP_LAYOUT RPC should also do early lock cancellation for the two files being swapped.

I also realized that there needs to be a sanityn.sh test that does the IO on one node, and swaps the layout on another node.  That will ensure that the handling of layout lock revocation and re-fetch work correctly when multiple nodes are involved.

Finally, please add a test for racer.sh that just swaps layouts of two random files.  This will test the layout lock revocation hard, as well as error handling when trying to swap layouts between different types of files:

 lustre/tests/racer/file_swap.sh:
 #!/bin/bash
 
 DIR=$1
 MAX=$2
 
 while /bin/true; do 
     file=$((RANDOM % MAX))
     new_file=$((RANDOM % MAX))
     lfs swap_layouts $DIR/$file $DIR/$new_file 2> /dev/null
 done

Patch Set 3: (1 inline comment)

Sorry, one more testing issue I thought of.

Patch Set 3: (13 inline comments)

New patch will follow

Patch Set 4:

Test 184 failure is "expected". After a layout swap a stat failed on the files with error ENOTOSUP. unmount/mount of the client resolves the issue and the files are well swapped. I suspect a layout lock issue, jinshan is looking at it.

Oleg, please run this patch for racer, since I don't want to land the added test if it immediately causes failures, since racer is not run as part of review (though it should be).

Patch Set 15: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(3 inline comments)

This patch conflict with one of the DNE patches recently landed, so it needs to be rebased.

Also I don't really like the new racer test, as it's too tooth-less.

I am about to run this test (with sleep and probably echo removed) overnight to see what happens too.

Patch Set 15:

as an addendum, that echo IS way too noisy when the sleep is removed.
It's also incorrect, because it prints when the swap failed (most of the time), but claims it succeeded.

Patch Set 15: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Oleg has had trouble passing any testing with the layout swap test in racer.  At the same time, this version of layout swap is not intended to be used for general-purpose migration, only for HSM doing layout swap of idle files.

Could you please resubmit the patch with Oleg's recommended changes, and comment out the patch so that it doesn't run during normal test runs.  Otherwise it will cause all racer testing to fail.  We can address the racer defects after the patch has landed.
Uploaded patch set 16.

parents.subject: LU-2728 fld: check for NULL param in fldb_seq_stop()

commit.subject: LU-2017 mdc: add layout swap between 2 objects
commit.message:
LU-2017 mdc: add layout swap between 2 objects

This patch adds the client and MDT code to swap layouts
between 2 files:
- a lfs command
- a llapi call, based on a new ioctl
- the mdc/mdt codes for the ioctl (with layout locking)

Signed-off-by: JC Lafoucriere <jacques-charles.lafoucriere@cea.fr>
Signed-off-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Change-Id: I7e710a1ab3ca38e8b26582e49d08e9943aa445cd
